prerequisites: A Fullstory account with permission to create API keys. The role you hold determines which key permission levels you can create: Standard, Architect, or Admin.
An API key generated under Settings > Integrations > API Keys in Fullstory, used by the connector as the api_key credential.
A uid value: the unique user identifier configured in your Fullstory script under FS.identify.

authMethods.name: API key
authMethods.summary: Authenticate with the api_key credential, generated in Fullstory under Settings > Integrations > API Keys. The connector uses it to call the Fullstory Server API. The uid credential identifies the specific user whose data is requested and corresponds to the application-specific identifier configured in your Fullstory script under FS.identify.

setupSteps.title: Create a Fullstory API key
setupSteps.body: In Fullstory, navigate to Settings > Integrations > API Keys and create a key. Enter a meaningful name and select a permission level your role allows (Standard, Architect, or Admin). Copy the generated key for use as the api_key credential.

setupSteps.title: Identify the uid
setupSteps.body: Determine the uid for the user whose data you want to pull. This is the application-specific unique identifier configured in your Fullstory script under FS.identify.

setupSteps.title: Configure the connector
setupSteps.body: Provide the api_key (your Fullstory API key) and the uid in the connector's required fields. Both are stored as secrets. The connector then authenticates to the Fullstory Server API using the API key.

sources.title: Getting Started - Server API V1 - Fullstory Developer Guide
Managing API Keys - Fullstory Help Center
